text.title: Cognitive Inclusion Design Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Cognitive Inclusion Design methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: User-facing copy targets Flesch Reading Ease ≥60 OR Flesch-Kincaid Grade ≤8 (whichever is stricter for the locale). Jargon usage requires inline definition.
rule.rationale: WCAG SC 3.1.5 explicitly cites reading-level metrics.

rule.statement: Persistent nav controls (header, footer, primary actions) MUST appear in the same relative location across pages within the flow.
rule.rationale: Users with cognitive disabilities rely on consistent placement.

rule.statement: Forms MUST identify errors inline, suggest concrete corrections, and preserve user input on validation fail.
rule.rationale: Error-clear-form patterns cause re-entry frustration; WCAG SC 3.3.1, 3.3.3, 3.3.4 apply.

rule.statement: Any critical action (delete, send, payment) MUST be undoable within a stated window OR confirmed via secondary step. Single-click destruction is forbidden.

rationale.source: WCAG SC 3.3.4 + Nielsen Norman Group recoverable-actions research

rule.statement: Any timeout MUST default to user-extendable (≥10× minimum window OR no timeout). Auto-logoffs without warning are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Time limits are a structural barrier for cognitive disabilities.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-cognitive-inclusion-design.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
